direction_head.model: StandardScaler plus multinomial logistic regression (lbfgs, C=1)
direction_head.representation: mean and population SD over g=2..16 for each of 27 non-depth coordinates; identical to the external-panel depth grid
direction_head.score_interpretation: uncalibrated out-of-distribution score; not a probability or posterior
direction_head.training_selection: all positive canonical replicates at every migration rate

gate_head.model: StandardScaler plus binary logistic regression (lbfgs, C=1)
gate_head.representation: selected depth rows plus across-depth mean of 27 non-depth coordinates
gate_head.score_interpretation: depth-matched uncalibrated OOD score; not the canonical 243-D gate probability
gate_head.training_target: positive migration with rate >=2.5e-4 versus weak positives plus control

guardrail: same-release, power-limited candidate-null crossing-sensitivity stress test only; no panel is a proven zero, gold label, independent validation, accuracy trial, or specificity trial

outcome_summary.interpretation: two correlated Georges River sites crossed with two shared-locus ascertainment filters; run counts are descriptive sensitivities, not validation trials

adjudication.interpretation_guardrail: the raw gate is not a probability or migration estimate; concordance/tension is descriptive and cannot be called a true negative, false positive, correct, or incorrect
adjudication.severe_OOD_rule: max(direction RMS-z, gate RMS-z) > 10; heuristic, not calibrated support

external_expectation.B2_founder_selection_guardrail: B2 is a selectively bred sixth-generation stock descended from 140 founders sampled across Georges River and three northern rivers; drift, pedigree, selection, and heterozygosity differ
external_expectation.joint_ascertainment: W and Q share one DArT discovery/filtering process and one anonymous locus release
external_expectation.joint_called_copy_guardrail: the shared minimum of 16 called copies allows at most one missing QBB2 genotype (n=9) versus four in n=12 cohorts, so the primary W panel is conditioned on Q missingness
external_expectation.locus_filter_variant: both alleles observed across each complete W and Q panel on one shared intersection
external_expectation.sample_scope: all released samples for this site after author preprocessing
external_expectation.source_label_reuse: the released 1,200-locus workbook derives from the same SNP-discovery data used by the paper, but already omits the three QBB2 individuals removed after DAPC and does not identify the paper's 1,189-neutral-locus subset; the source label/exclusion is outcome-derived from the same underlying data, not independent

input_audit.comparison_locus_contract: same exact ordered locus set as the other Georges River site within this filter

filter_contract.cap_method: fixed-seed reservoir over eligible loci, restored to source order
filter_contract.filter: PASS or dot (sources are author-filtered releases)
filter_contract.polymorphism: both REF and ALT must be called across every declared three-population panel
filter_contract.variant: single-base REF and single-base ALT; no multiallelic ALT

input_audit.joint_called_copy_guardrail: minimum 16 called copies in every one of six cohorts means QBB2 (n=9) may miss at most one diploid genotype per locus while n=12 cohorts may miss four; because loci are shared, W is also conditioned on Q missingness
input_audit.shared_site_contract: one exact ordered W/Q locus intersection; site outputs are directly paired within this filter

iid_locus_bootstrap.guardrail: fixed-sample conditional sensitivity only; the workbook supplies no chromosome, physical position, or linkage map, so independence is unverified and this is not chromosome-block uncertainty
iid_locus_bootstrap.method: naive IID resampling of anonymous DArT loci

model_free_comparator.interpretation: sample-frequency geometry only. Projection is reference-flip invariant but is not bounded ancestry, migration, or temporal direction. The finite-called-copy f3 subtraction assumes independent binomial called-copy sampling and is not generally unbiased; positive f3 does not prove a near-null and negative f3 would not supply direction. The prespecified 0.95 diagnostic threshold is never tuned post hoc.

simulation_feature_shift.reference: StandardScaler fitted to 2,700 positive regen_full summaries on the identical g=2..16 grid

simulation_gate.interpretation: uncalibrated gate-classifier score on a natural-data input; not a probability, posterior, migration estimate, or OOD-detector score

simulation_head.interpretation: uncalibrated classifier scores on a natural-data input; not probabilities, posteriors, or OOD-detector scores

published_evidence.Q_same_data_exclusions: original IDs 28, 29, and 31 were omitted from released QBB2 after the paper's DAPC on its 1,189 neutral loci assigned them to the wild/control cluster; Q is outcome-conditioned and secondary
published_evidence.detection_limit: site sample sizes of 9-12 and same-data preprocessing may miss low gene flow, occasional hybrids, or gene flow that does not produce sustained introgression; the finding does not establish migration rate zero

later_context.reanalysis_search_status: Dryad/DataCite relations and exact DOI/filename searches located no later independent reanalysis of the released 90-by-1,200 matrix as of 2026-07-11
later_context.review: Bishop et al. 2023 (10.3389/fmars.2023.1162487) is a review that cites Thompson 2017 among studies reporting little evidence of aquaculture-line introgression; it performs no genotype reanalysis and supplies no independent truth label
later_context.species_wide_generalization_guardrail: the 2021 result of one highly connected wild stock, high effective population size, and no recent bottleneck argues against generalizing the 2017 Georges River inference to compromised species-wide wild-population resilience; it does not re-test or overturn the local wild-versus-B2 diversity contrast
later_context.wild_population_study: O'Hare et al. 2021 (10.1007/s10592-021-01343-4) analyzed a different 363-wild-oyster/3,400-neutral-SNP question, contains no B2 or overcatch cohorts, and is not a documented reanalysis; O'Hare is the same lead researcher as Thompson and Stow/Raftos are shared coauthors, so it is not independent validation

published_evidence.source_result: strong wild-versus-B2 partitioning and no detected sustained introgression at either site
published_evidence.topology_guardrail: WC and overcatch are nearby wild microhabitat cohorts, while B2 is selected hatchery stock; the operational ((WC,OC),B2) order is not a rooted species tree

runtime.process_priority: BelowNormal requested at runner start on Windows

source.ascertainment_guardrails: anonymous DArT genotype-by-sequencing release; 1,189 neutral plus 11 consensus selection outliers cannot be distinguished by the generic locus labels; W/Q share joint discovery; released QBB2 already omits three oysters removed after the paper's neutral-locus DAPC; physical linkage is unavailable

excluded_nonbenchmark_reference_cohorts.reason: HB2 and HC are useful stock/reference cohorts but form only a two-group hatchery comparison and do not supply a defensible third population for the DNNaic topology

rows.guardrail: Two PSH cohorts do not form a defensible three-population DNNaic topology.
Same founder-generation context is useful biologically but not an independent third population.
Original IDs 28, 29, and 31 were omitted after the paper's DAPC on its 1,189 neutral loci assigned them to the wild/control cluster.
Wild overcatch is a nearby microhabitat cohort, not a temporal descendant population.
Q shares stock, sampling date, joint SNP ascertainment, and source inference with W.
B2 is a sixth-generation selected/founder stock, not a species-tree outgroup.
Candidate exposure orientation if an event exists is P3 to P2 (class C), not a positive truth label.
The source near-null is same-data and detection-limited, not proof of zero gene flow.

filter_contract.cap_method: fixed-seed reservoir over eligible loci, restored to source order
filter_contract.filter: PASS or dot (sources are author-filtered releases)
filter_contract.polymorphism: both REF and ALT must be called across every declared three-population panel
filter_contract.variant: single-base REF and single-base ALT; no multiallelic ALT

standard_contract.joint_called_copy_guardrail: minimum 16 called copies in every one of six cohorts means QBB2 (n=9) may miss at most one diploid genotype per locus while n=12 cohorts may miss four; because loci are shared, W is also conditioned on Q missingness
standard_contract.shared_site_contract: one exact ordered W/Q locus intersection; site outputs are directly paired within this filter

filter_contract.cap_method: fixed-seed reservoir over eligible loci, restored to source order
filter_contract.filter: PASS or dot (sources are author-filtered releases)
filter_contract.polymorphism: both REF and ALT must be called within every population of every declared panel
filter_contract.variant: single-base REF and single-base ALT; no multiallelic ALT

within_population_polymorphism.joint_called_copy_guardrail: minimum 16 called copies in every one of six cohorts means QBB2 (n=9) may miss at most one diploid genotype per locus while n=12 cohorts may miss four; because loci are shared, W is also conditioned on Q missingness
within_population_polymorphism.shared_site_contract: one exact ordered W/Q locus intersection; site outputs are directly paired within this filter

analysis_design.Q: secondary post-hoc-cleaned sensitivity: QWC=P1, QOC=P2, QB2=P3
analysis_design.W: primary cleaner near-null comparison: WWC=P1, WOC=P2, WB2=P3
analysis_design.minimum_called_copies_guardrail: The shared minimum of 16 called copies lets QBB2 (n=9) miss at most one diploid genotype per locus while n=12 cohorts may miss four; the shared W/Q intersection therefore conditions W on Q missingness.

known_release_discrepancies: The paper states that individuals and loci were retained at at least 95% call rate, but the released final workbook has 9 individuals and 387 loci below 95%; all individuals remain above 94%.

A site-only W standard prefilter happens to retain 1,189 loci, numerically matching the paper's neutral count, but the released generic locus labels cannot establish that it is the same neutral subset; the paired benchmark instead freezes a shared W/Q intersection.
The Dryad usage note says workbook population codes match the manuscript, while the workbook uses PSH/QB/WB prefixes that require an explicit crosswalk to HB/HC/Q/W paper codes.

restoration_review_2023.interpretation: Cites Thompson 2017 among studies reporting little evidence of aquaculture-line introgression but performs no genotype reanalysis; dependent synthesis only.
restoration_review_2023.title: Oyster reef restoration - aquaculture interactions: maximizing positive synergies

later_context.search_status_as_of_2026_07_11: Dryad/DataCite relations and exact DOI/filename searches located no later independent reanalysis or benchmark label for the released 90-by-1,200 matrix.

wild_connectivity_2021.interpretation: Different question and release; O'Hare is the same lead researcher as Thompson, and Stow/Raftos are shared coauthors; not a documented reanalysis or independent benchmark. Its broad connectivity, high effective population size, and no-recent-bottleneck result argues against generalizing the 2017 Georges River inference to compromised species-wide wild-population resilience; it does not re-test or overturn the local wild-versus-B2 diversity contrast.
wild_connectivity_2021.scope: 363 definitive wild S. glomerata and 3,400 neutral SNPs across 25 estuaries; no B2 or overcatch cohort
wild_connectivity_2021.title: Genetic structure and effective population size of Sydney rock oysters in eastern Australia

paper.guardrail: The reported failure to detect sustained introgression is same-data and power-limited; it is not proof that migration equals zero.
paper.title: Lack of genetic introgression between wild and selectively bred Sydney rock oysters Saccostrea glomerata

population_mapping.crosswalk_status: mechanical workbook-code to paper-code mapping verified against row order, counts, and source methods

workbook_contract.allele_encoding: 0/0 missing; 1 is the workbook reference code; 2 is the workbook SNP code
workbook_contract.identity_guardrail: All loci are generic LocusN labels. The 1,189 neutral and 11 consensus selection-outlier loci cannot be distinguished from the release.

verified.retrieval_contract: only the canonical inner workbook is recorded; acquisition route and generated ZIP-wrapper presence, bytes, timestamp, size, and digest are deliberately excluded from result identity

workbook_audit.locus_identity_guardrail: the workbook labels loci only as Locus1..Locus1200 and does not identify which 11 were consensus selection outliers; the paper's 1,189 neutral-locus subset cannot be reconstructed
workbook_audit.paper_filter_discrepancy: the paper states individuals and loci were retained at >=95% call rate, but the final released workbook has 9 individuals and 387 loci below 95%; all individuals remain above 94%
